content.content: The file C:\Users\kisde\Desktop\raven\sandbox\med-baseline-1\src\strings.js has been updated successfully.

lines: + * Converts a string into a URL-friendly slug.
+ * Lowercases, trims, replaces non-alphanumeric runs with hyphens,
+ * @param {*} input - The value to slugify. Coerced to string via `String()`.
+ * Truncates a string to `max` characters, appending an ellipsis ("ΓÇª")
+ * when the string exceeds the limit. The ellipsis occupies one character
+ * @param {*} input - The value to truncate. Coerced to string via `String()`.
+ * @param {number} max - Maximum length of the returned string (including ellipsis).
+ * @returns {string} The original string if it fits, otherwise the truncated string with "ΓÇª".
+ * Capitalizes the first character of the given string.
+ * @param {*} input - The value to capitalize. Coerced to string via `String()`.
+ * @returns {string} The string with its first character uppercased.

content.content: The file C:\Users\kisde\Desktop\raven\sandbox\med-baseline-1\src\arrays.js has been updated successfully.

lines: + * Splits an array into chunks of the given size.
+ * Returns an empty array when `size` is zero or negative.
+ * @param {number} size - Desired chunk size (must be > 0 for output).
+ * @returns {Array<Array>} A new array of sub-arrays, each at most `size` long.
+ * Returns a new array with duplicate values removed, preserving first-occurrence order.
+ * @returns {Array} A new array containing only unique values.
+ * Groups array elements by a key derived from each element via `keyFn`.
+ * @param {function(*): string} keyFn - Function that returns a group key for each element.
+ * @returns {Object<string, Array>} An object mapping each key to an array of items.

+ * Rounds a number to a specified number of decimal digits.
+ * Checks whether a number falls within the inclusive range [`lo`, `hi`].

lines: + * Returns a new object containing only the specified keys from the source.
+ * Keys not present in `obj` are silently skipped.
+ * @returns {Object} A new object with only the picked key-value pairs.
for (const k of keys) if (k in obj) out[k] = obj[k];
+ * Returns a new object with the specified keys omitted from the source.
+ * @returns {Object} A new object without the omitted keys.
+ * Checks whether an object is `null`, `undefined`, or has no own enumerable keys.
+ * @returns {boolean} `true` when the value is nullish or has no keys.

content.content: The file C:\Users\kisde\Desktop\raven\sandbox\med-baseline-1\src\dates.js has been updated successfully.

lines: + * Formats a date as an ISO 8601 string (e.g. `"2024-06-15T12:30:00.000Z"`).
+ * @param {Date|string|number} date - A value accepted by the `Date` constructor.
+ * @returns {string} The ISO string representation of the date.
+ * Returns a new `Date` that is `days` days after the given date (UTC).
+ * Use a negative `days` value to subtract days.
+ * @param {Date|string|number} date - A value accepted by the `Date` constructor.
+ * @param {number} days - Number of days to add (can be negative).
+ * Computes the whole-day difference between two dates (`b ΓêÆ a`).
+ * Returns the floor of the millisecond difference divided by 86 400 000.
+ * @returns {number} Number of whole days between the two dates (can be negative).
const ms = new Date(b).getTime() - new Date(a).getTime();

content.content: The file C:\Users\kisde\Desktop\raven\sandbox\med-baseline-1\src\predicates.js has been updated successfully.

lines: + * Checks whether a string is a valid e-mail address.
+ * Uses a simple regex: one `@`, at least one `.` after `@`, no whitespace.
+ * @returns {boolean} `true` if `s` looks like a valid e-mail.
+ * Checks whether a string is a valid http(s) URL.
+ * @returns {boolean} `true` if `s` is a parseable URL with `http:` or `https:` protocol.
+ * Checks whether a string is a valid UUID (versions 1-5, variants 8/9/a/b).
